caption: The video appears to be a presentation slide discussing the collaboration between OPS-5G and the Linux Foundation. The slide is titled "OPS-5G Collaboration with the Linux Foundation" and highlights that OPS-5G performers will be contributing directly to various Linux Foundation projects. A table on the slide lists several LF Projects, their respective performers, and the current status of each project. For instance, ONAP is being worked on by USC ISI with active discussions ongoing, while OPEN Daylight involves GATech showing interest in an OpenFlow code upgrade. Other projects like DPDK, FD.io, and Zephyr have been briefed regarding the Developer Certificate of Origin (DCO). EMCO / Intel Smart Edge Open is noted as not being an LF project. The presenter, visible in a small window on the top right corner, seems to be explaining these details, likely elaborating on the contributions and statuses of each project. The bottom of the slide includes hashtags and logos for The Linux Foundation, LF Networking, and LF Edge, along with a distribution statement indicating public release approval.
